cards.name: Spark60: Meditations for Mortals: The Liberating Art of Embracing Your Limits with Oliver Burkeman
cards.blurb: Looking for a spark of inspiration? Spark60 is your monthly passport to big ideas and bestselling thinkers. In this edition, hear from Oliver Burkeman .
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Wander through the Triton's current exhibitions and spend time in the sculpture garden while the morning light is still soft. Free admission means you can linger as long as you want without any guilt.
cards.why: It's one of the most underrated art spaces in the South Bay and perfectly complements the headspace from this morning's talk.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Mix and match from the vendors at San Pedro Square Market. Grab tacos from one spot, something from the ramen counter, and find a patio seat to eat it all.
cards.why: It's the most fun way to do lunch when you can't decide what you're craving.

cards.name: AI-Powered tools to help with your searching
cards.blurb: Drop into this free session on AI-powered search tools and pick up a few tricks that'll actually save you time on a regular basis.
cards.why: Free, practical, and the kind of thing you leave actually knowing something new.

cards.name: Back & Body Wellness
cards.address: 4675 Stevens Creek Blvd # 200, Santa Clara, CA 95051, USA
cards.blurb: Book a session at Back and Body Wellness and let the day wind down properly with some hands-on recovery work.
cards.why: After a full morning and afternoon, this is a genuinely good use of the early evening.

cards.name: Philz Coffee
cards.address: 118 Paseo de San Antonio, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Start the day with a custom-blended pour at Philz — tell them your vibe (strong, sweet, somewhere in between) and they'll make it right.
cards.why: Best way to ease into a Thursday morning before things pick up.

cards.name: San Jose Municipal Rose Garden
cards.address: Municipal Rose Garden
cards.blurb: Walk the paths through hundreds of rose varieties — it's peak spring and the blooms are going off right now.
cards.why: 73 degrees and roses in April is a pretty ideal combo.

cards.name: SoSo Meeting at Dudley Moorhead Hall
cards.blurb: Hit up Swana Sweets at the SJSU Student Union for whatever they're serving up today — outdoor, free, and a fun campus-energy stop.
cards.why: It's locked in and it's a solid midmorning sweet break.

cards.name: Swana Sweets at Student Union
cards.blurb: Join us for “SWANA Sweets”, a community gathering to celebrate the start of Southwest Asian & North African (SWANA) Heritage Month! We’ll have yummy SWANA desserts and snacks and a collaborative &…
cards.why: You picked this one.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Hit San Pedro Square Market for lunch and graze your way through the stalls. Tacos, banh mi, ramen, whatever sounds good — grab a patio seat and settle in.
cards.why: You can mix and match from a dozen vendors without committing to one thing, which is exactly right for a weekday lunch.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Wander the Triton Museum of Art for free contemporary art and a sculpture garden out back. Takes about two hours at a relaxed pace.
cards.why: It's genuinely underrated and the outdoor sculpture garden is perfect on a day like this.

cards.name: AI-Powered tools to help with your searching
cards.blurb: Catch the AI-Powered Tools for Your Search session at SCU — it's free and actually useful if you want to walk away knowing something new.
cards.why: Free, practical, and fills the afternoon window perfectly.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Santana Row for the evening stretch. Browse the boutiques, people-watch from a patio, and grab dinner at one of the restaurants along the boulevard.
cards.why: It's the right vibe for a Thursday night when the weather is this good.

cards.name: Jackie's Place - North
cards.address: 840 N First St, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Start the day at Jackie's Place North with a solid breakfast before the city gets going — solid eggs, good coffee, the kind of spot locals actually go to.
cards.why: A proper sit-down breakfast sets the tone for a full day out.

cards.name: Friday Fun : Legos® Fun
cards.blurb: Join us every Friday at the Willow Glen Branch Library for a fun activity for kids. Activities may include arts & crafts, special performances or workshops, and board or video games. Note: 2nd Friday…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Patient No More Exhibition at King Library
cards.blurb: The Patient No More Exhibition at King Library is free and worth a wander — compelling visual storytelling you can take at your own pace.
cards.why: Free art on a Thursday morning is a pretty solid way to ease into the day.

cards.name: Almaden Lake Park
cards.address: 6099 Winfield Blvd, San Jose, CA 95120, USA
cards.blurb: Head over to Almaden Lake Park for a relaxed late-morning walk around the lake while it's still cool and uncrowded.
cards.why: That 73°F high makes this the perfect window to be outside before it peaks.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: San Pedro Square Market is the move for lunch — grab a banh mi from one vendor, a coffee from another, and post up at a patio table with no agenda.
cards.why: Mix-and-match lunch spots don't get better than this in downtown San Jose.

cards.name: Restful SPA
cards.address: 991 Saratoga Ave #150, San Jose, CA 95129, USA
cards.blurb: Book a session at Restful SPA for a mid-afternoon reset — massages, facials, the works. Great way to spend a Thursday afternoon guilt-free.
cards.why: Adults-only day deserves a proper wind-down stretch in the middle of it.

cards.name: Wagyu Master Sukiyaki House | San Jose
cards.address: 1410 El Paseo de Saratoga, San Jose, CA 95130, USA
cards.blurb: Wagyu Master Sukiyaki House is a full experience — you're cooking premium wagyu in a hot pot at the table, so take your time and enjoy every bite.
cards.why: This is the kind of dinner that makes a regular Thursday feel like something special.

cards.name: San Jose Giants vs. Visalia Rawhide
cards.address: 588 E Alma Ave, San Jose
cards.blurb: Catch the San Jose Giants vs. Visalia Rawhide at Excite Ballpark — grab a beer, settle into your seat, and enjoy minor league baseball the way it's meant to be watched.
cards.why: Evening games at this ballpark are genuinely fun and tickets start at $14.

cards.name: ZombieRunner Coffee
cards.address: 344 California Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at ZombieRunner, a California Ave institution that does great coffee in a no-frills, runner-friendly space. Grab a cup and settle in before the day gets going.
cards.why: Solid coffee and a chill vibe to ease into a packed Thursday.

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.address: Cantor Arts Center, Stanford University
cards.blurb: Wander through the Cantor Arts Center on Stanford's campus. Spend time in the galleries, then step outside to the Rodin Sculpture Garden — the outdoor collection alone is worth the trip.
cards.why: Free world-class art on a crisp spring morning is a genuinely good way to spend two hours.

cards.name: Kali Greek Kitchen
cards.address: 451 California Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Kali Greek Kitchen on California Ave for lunch. The menu leans fresh and Mediterranean, so order whatever looks good and grab a table outside if the weather holds.
cards.why: California Ave is a great midday spot and Kali is one of the better lunch options on the strip.

cards.name: The Dish Trail
cards.address: Stanford Foothills (Stanford Ave trailhead)
cards.blurb: Work off lunch on The Dish Trail. The loop climbs into the Stanford foothills with wide open views of the Bay and the signature satellite dish overhead. Wear comfortable shoes and bring water.
cards.why: 73°F and sunny is basically perfect hiking weather for this one.

cards.name: 1,000 Ways to Hold: A New Work By Erika Chong Shuch
cards.address: 314 Lomita Dr, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Catch '1,000 Ways to Hold,' a new work by Erika Chong Shuch at Stanford. It's a free arts event, so walk in and see what the piece is doing — performance art like this tends to hit different when you're not expecting much.
cards.why: Free, thought-provoking, and a nice cool-down after the hike.

cards.name: Thai Smile Bodywork & Massage
cards.address: 467 Hamilton Ave Suite 26, 2nd Floor, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Book a session at Thai Smile Bodywork and Massage on Hamilton Ave. After a full day on your feet, a proper massage is a smart call before the evening wraps up.
cards.why: Your legs will thank you after The Dish.

cards.name: Dinah's Poolside Restaurant
cards.address: 4261 El Camino Real, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at Dinah's Poolside Restaurant. Order something from the full menu, grab a spot near the pool area, and let the evening slow down. It's a solid dinner spot with real atmosphere.
cards.why: A relaxed poolside dinner is a great cap to a day this full.

cards.name: Ccrma Presents: MediaFlock 2026 – Multimedia Art Festival
cards.address: 660 Lomita Court, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: MediaFlock 2026 is a one-day art festival featuring multimedia art pieces from CCRMA students and guest artists from other California universities. Join us on April 10th to celebrate 30+ pieces of…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Knit & Crochet Circle at Milpitas Library
cards.blurb: Mend with Friends — a family gathering in Milpitas. Show up, connect, and enjoy the company.
cards.why: It's already locked in, so just lean into it.

cards.name: Mend with Friends
cards.blurb: Do you have... ...a stuffed animal with the stuffing leaking out? ...a shirt with a missing button? ...a hole you want to patch? ...a stain you want to cover up? Bring it to the Mend with Friends…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.

cards.name: Sinoy's Kitchen
cards.address: 283 Jacklin Rd, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Sinoy's Kitchen and dig into whatever's fresh off the menu — this little spot in Milpitas punches well above its weight for a casual morning bite.
cards.why: Good food first thing sets the whole day up right.

cards.name: Intel Museum
cards.address: 2200 Mission College Blvd, Santa Clara, CA 95054, USA
cards.blurb: Head over to the Intel Museum in Santa Clara and geek out on the history of computing, chip innovation, and how Silicon Valley became what it is today — free and genuinely fascinating.
cards.why: It's a surprisingly deep museum and a great way to spend a couple hours.

cards.name: Mikonos Grill
cards.address: 756 E Calaveras Blvd, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at Mikonos Grill on Calaveras and work through their Mediterranean menu — think grilled meats, fresh flavors, and a solid midday meal that won't leave you sluggish.
cards.why: It's a great lunch spot and the food holds up every time.

cards.name: TD Beauty Spa
cards.address: 56 S Abel St, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Book a treatment at TD Beauty Spa on S Abel St and actually decompress for a couple hours before the evening kicks off.
cards.why: A midafternoon spa visit hits different when you have nowhere to be yet.

cards.name: Sugar Mama Desserts
cards.address: Ranch 99 Shopping Center, 492 Barber Ln, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by Sugar Mama Desserts at Ranch 99 and treat yourself to something sweet to cap the afternoon before dinner plans take shape.
cards.why: Dessert before dinner is a valid life choice and this place earns it.

cards.name: Mikonos Grill
cards.address: 756 E Calaveras Blvd, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the night at Fairway Zone Sports Bar and Lounge in San Jose — grab a drink, catch whatever's on, and let the evening go easy.
cards.why: A chill bar with a good vibe is the right way to finish a Thursday.

cards.name: Ethel's Fancy
cards.address: 550 Waverley St, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Start the day at Ethel's Fancy with a morning coffee and something from their rotating menu of thoughtful, locally-sourced bites.
cards.why: It's one of those spots that actually makes you want to linger over your coffee.

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.address: Cantor Arts Center, Stanford University
cards.blurb: Wander through the free galleries and spend some time in the outdoor Rodin Sculpture Garden. The mix of indoor exhibitions and the open-air courtyard makes it an easy, genuinely rewarding morning.
cards.why: World-class art that's completely free and never feels crowded on a weekday morning.

cards.name: Spotlight Tours Thursdays | Anderson Collection
cards.address: 314 Lomita Dr, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Join the Thursday Spotlight Tour at the Anderson Collection for a focused, docent-led look at a key work or theme in the collection. It's a compact, satisfying way to see the art with context.
cards.why: Free, right on Stanford's campus, and way more interesting than just walking the galleries solo.

cards.name: Graphic Novel Book Club
cards.blurb: Head to the Graphic Novel Book Club. Good conversation, interesting picks, and the kind of crowd that takes comics seriously without being precious about it.
cards.why: It's a fun midday social break that doesn't feel like homework.

cards.name: Dinah's Poolside Restaurant
cards.address: 4261 El Camino Real, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Settle in at Dinah's Poolside Restaurant for a proper lunch. The patio is great on a day like this, so grab a table outside and order something substantial.
cards.why: Lunch with a poolside patio in 73-degree weather is hard to beat.

cards.name: Avy Spa Massage
cards.address: 3783 El Camino Real, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Book an afternoon massage at Avy Spa and actually decompress for a bit. Walk-in availability may vary so call ahead.
cards.why: Best way to spend the slow part of the afternoon.

cards.name: Rootstock Wine Bar
cards.address: 331 Castro St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at Rootstock Wine Bar with a glass of something interesting and a few small plates. The list skews toward natural and lesser-known producers, so ask the staff what they're excited about.
cards.why: Low-key, genuinely good wine, and exactly the right vibe for winding down a full day.

cards.name: Teaching Commons Conference 2026: Courageous Curiosity
cards.blurb: Teaching Commons Conference Spring 2026 The Teaching Commons Conference is a free, in-person event held on campus every spring that brings together people from across Stanford University around…
cards.why: This is the one the day is built around.
